text: Okay, now timestamps matter, but don't code it yet. A duplicate id should only be considered a duplicate if it is within five minutes of the earlier accepted event.
Five minutes means 300 seconds if timestamps are integer seconds, right?
Yes. Assume the events arrive sorted by timestamp for now. I know that's a big assumption, but keep it simple.
So I need to expire old ids as I move forward, otherwise an id from ten minutes ago would still block a new event. I won't use deque yet unless you want optimization.
Exactly, just explain that plan. We'll implement after.
